transcript:
SOURCE: Fathom (via HubSpot internal notes) — https://fathom.video/share/xxxqWFxiSBryz4N9yUUvqthyXDGpPq1n

AI Meeting Summary ✨

Generated by Fathom // View Recording (64 mins - No highlights)

Deal Information

Prospect

Scott Holt representing NYC Estate Plans (estate planning law firm in Jamaica, Queens, NY). Kim is the attorney/face of firm; Scott handles marketing/ops. They also have a receptionist and office manager; use non-attorney salespeople for first meetings, Kim joins second meeting. Considering a second office not yet live on Maps.

Call Context

Reconnect and account setup working session focused on marketing performance and intake process. Triggered by prior convo about improving organic/AI visibility and tightening intake. Live review uncovered website rendering anomalies on Dan’s devices.

Seats

N/A

Budget

Current PPC ad spend: increasing from ~$6k to ~$8k/month. Historical LSA spend active. No explicit total budget cap stated.

Current State

Channels: Google LSA active; Google Ads (PPC) run by “Grow My Ads.” No social ads. Organic presence weak.

Website: nycestateplans.com; Scott deems it “crap,” over-weighted to probate (which they don’t want). No current web vendor.

Intake: CallRail numbers; routed into GoHighLevel (GHL) manually (auto-creation disabled due to bad caller ID data). Phones on RingCentral. No 24/7 answering; previous virtual receptionist vendors disappointed.

Locations: Primary in Jamaica, Queens; second location exists but not live/optimized on Maps/site.

Pain Points

Organic visibility gap: Low rankings across target grid; home/meta/H1 misalignment and thin/local relevance.

Website quality and messaging: Site over-indexed on probate (undesired), poor optimization and content depth; likely hurting conversion and AI discoverability.

Intake leakage: Missed calls after-hours; no automated CRM lead creation from calls; risk of abandoned/short calls not being worked.

LSA performance variance: Competitive market favors 24/7 and message leads; responsiveness and reviews critical; suspect untapped potential.

Fragmented marketing ops: PPC-only vendor won’t touch LPs/LSA; Scott forced to DIY landing pages causing inconsistency and fatigue.

Specific Requirements

Improve AI/organic discoverability: Scott wants “AI to know us” and authoritative local content aligned to estate planning (not probate).

Tighten intake workflow: After-hours handling that books 10-minute next-day intakes with reminders and a pre-call video; ensure calls/leads drop into GHL with accurate data hygiene.

Preserve effective PPC while enhancing conversion: Willing to have SMB Team audit Ads; open to better landing pages if compatible with current PPC setup.

Local dominance: Optimize GMB for Queens/Jamaica; structure site for multi-location with practice-area silos per office; embed maps; expand hours strategically.

Tech Stack

Call tracking: CallRail

CRM/Marketing automation: GoHighLevel (GHL)

Phones: RingCentral

Ads: Google LSA; Google Ads via Grow My Ads

Calendaring: Open to Calendly/HubSpot-style scheduling for intakes

Website CMS: Not specified; current site underperforms

Objections

Virtual receptionist skepticism: Negative experiences with Smith.ai and Backup Betty’s (billing/hold times, poor scripting). Not resolved; Dan proposed limited overflow/nights-weekends plus tight script and booking workflow.

Vendor boundaries: PPC vendor won’t handle LPs/LSA; Scott wary of gaps. Dan open to collaboration and potential LP build if included, but atypical; to confirm internally. Not fully resolved.

LSA “not what it used to be”: Market shifts since 2024; Scott saw quality/volume declines. Dan addressed with benchmarks, controls (hours, no messages), and responsiveness/review strategy. Partially addressed.

Other Solutions Under Evaluation

Current: Grow My Ads (PPC). Prior: Non-Stuffy Attorney (web/SEO; terminated), Ruby/Lex Reception/Smith.ai/Backup Betty’s mentioned historically.

Timeline

Immediate next steps: Provide Google Ads read-only access for SMB Team audit; follow-up meeting scheduled Wed the 15th at 11:00 (ET) for findings and proposal. Second office to be brought live post-plan.

Decision Maker

Scott operational/marketing lead; Kim is principal attorney and firm figurehead. Scott indicated Kim “likes the idea.” Final decision likely shared, with Scott leading.

Procurement Process

Collaborative evaluation by Scott and Kim; Scott grants platform access and coordinates. Preference to start where ROI is fastest (LSA/SEO/intake), keep PPC if performing, and add services modularly.

Next steps

Scott to grant Google Ads read-only to:

ppcteam@smbteam.com

Nick (email provided on call; Dan to confirm)

SMB Team to complete PPC audit (allow 3–4 days) and prepare recommendations.

Dan to propose plan prioritizing:

Website redesign + SEO (local practice-area silos per location; 20–30k words; Queens/Jamaica focus; reduce probate emphasis)

LSA optimization (hours strategy; responsiveness tracking; review loop)

Intake workflow: after-hours overflow script, booking links, reminders, pre-call video guidance; CallRail→GHL lead creation with caller ID cleanup

Optional remarketing on Meta for site/LP visitors ($500–$600/mo starter)

Follow-up call: Wed the 15th at 11:00 ET to review audit and start with “the pitch.”

Q&A

Questions We Asked

Who manages the website and is anyone currently in charge?

No one presently; prior vendor “Non-Stuffy Attorney” built it; Scott considers it poor and misaligned (too much probate).

Are LSAs performing and how are they configured (hours/messages)?

They run LSAs; exact numbers unclear. Competitors run 24/7 with message leads; Dan advised against messages and to match hours to live answer capability.

What software stack do you use (CRM, call tracking, case mgmt)?

GHL + CallRail; RingCentral phones; internal spreadsheet for case management (no dedicated CMS).

Do CallRail calls auto-create leads in GHL?

No; disabled due to dirty caller ID populating bad names. Open to revisit with data hygiene.

Do you have 24/7 answering or overflow coverage?

No; past issues with Smith.ai and Backup Betty’s (hold times, odd questions). Open to structured overflow with strict script and booking.

Current PPC status and vendor?

Active; ~$6k moving to $8k; vendor is Grow My Ads (not legal-specific). They do not manage LPs or LSA.

Ownership of ad accounts?

Scott owns all accounts. Open to giving SMB Team read-only for audit.

Practice area focus and expansion?

Estate planning primary; maybe elder law later. Intend to avoid probate.

Questions They Asked

How to operationalize after-hours booking flow?

Dan outlined: receptionist books 10-minute next-day intake via Calendly/HubSpot; immediate SMS/email reminders with a short video from Kim; goal is to capture, pre-sell, and reduce no-shows; keep script minimal (matter type, location, contact) and avoid legal advice.

Should the pre-call video feature Kim or non-attorney salesperson?

Start with Kim for authority. Frame expectations that first call is with her trained team member; Kim joins second meeting. If needed, replicate video with salesperson later.

Can SMB Team create PPC landing pages if another vendor runs ads?

Not typical, but potentially if included in package; Dan to confirm internally. Regardless, SMB Team can pixel pages for remarketing and collaborate with existing PPC.

Can we start next meeting with “the pitch”?

Yes; Dan will review audit and tailor proposal; plan to open with recommendations aligned to goals.

Action Items

Fix CallRail→GHL integration: ensure leads created; correct caller ID - WATCH (5 secs)
Grant Google Ads read-only access to PPC Team (ppcteam@smbteam.com) + Nick; domain trust; 3–4d window - WATCH (5 secs)
Send calendar invite to Scott for Jul 15 11:00–12:00 ET - WATCH (5 secs)
